name: Generator Skill
description: Ship the @relay/generator Claude Code skill: the SKILL.md that drives the interactive flow scaffolder, four starter templates, an installer that copies the skill into ~/.claude/skills/, and the scaffolding logic that customizes a template for the user's request. The generator does not run flows — it only emits new Flow Packages per §7.

waves.name: Generator SKILL.md
waves.description: Create `packages/generator/skill/SKILL.md`. Follows the Claude Code skill format with frontmatter (name: relay-generator, description mentioning the triggers from §6.2: 'scaffold a new relay flow', '/relay-new', 'generate a pipeline for ...', tools: Read, Write, AskUserQuestion, Bash). Body walks the user through §6.2 steps: name the flow (kebab-case validation), pick a template (blank/linear/fan-out/discovery — match against user intent or AskUserQuestion), elicit high-level steps, choose model per step (default sonnet), then Write the generated files. Reference the template picker script (task_61) via Bash. Include the §7.1 directory layout as the output contract. Include a 'what this skill does NOT do' section mirroring §6.3.

waves.name: Template: blank
waves.description: Fill `packages/generator/templates/blank/` with a minimal Flow Package per §7.1. Files (all use {{placeholder}} tokens that the scaffolding logic replaces): `package.json` (name: {{pkgName}}, version: 0.1.0, deps on @relay/core as peer, the §7.2 relay metadata block), `flow.ts` (imports defineFlow + step + z from @relay/core; one prompt step reading `prompts/01_first.md`, output handoff: 'result'; input schema: z.object({ subject: z.string() })), `prompts/01_first.md` (1-2 sentence placeholder that references {{input.subject}}), `tsconfig.json` extending @relay/core's, `README.md` stubbed with the §7.4 required sections pre-filled for the user to edit.

waves.name: Template: linear
waves.description: Fill `packages/generator/templates/linear/` — three prompt steps wired in series (step1 → step2 → step3). Each prompt file has a concise placeholder referencing the prior handoff via `{{prevHandoff.*}}`. flow.ts uses step.prompt with `dependsOn` + `contextFrom`. Same package.json / tsconfig / README structure as blank. Tokens: {{pkgName}}, {{stepNames[*]}}.

waves.name: Template: fan-out
waves.description: Fill `packages/generator/templates/fan-out/` — a fan-out/fan-in pattern: one prep step → two parallel branches → a single reducer step that consumes both branch handoffs. flow.ts uses step.prompt for the four prompt steps and step.parallel for the fan-in barrier. Standard package.json / tsconfig / README.

waves.name: Template: discovery
waves.description: Fill `packages/generator/templates/discovery/` — modeled after the canonical codebase-discovery flow (Appendix A). Four prompt steps (inventory → entities + services in parallel → report) with the Zod schemas inlined in `schemas/inventory.ts` and `schemas/entities.ts`. Prompts are token-rich placeholders explaining the intent. Primary artifact is `report.html`. Use this template for intents matching 'explore', 'audit', 'document', 'review codebase'. Standard package.json / tsconfig / README.

waves.name: Template scaffolding engine
waves.description: Create `packages/generator/src/scaffold.ts` exporting `scaffoldFlow({ template, outDir, tokens })`. Copies every file from `packages/generator/templates/<template>/` into `outDir` (resolve via `import.meta.url` since this runs from the installed skill location). For each file, replace `{{tokens}}` by value. Preserve sub-directory structure. Refuse to overwrite existing files unless tokens.force === true. Return a summary of files written. Also export `pickTemplate(intentText: string): 'blank'|'linear'|'fan-out'|'discovery'` — a tiny rule-based heuristic (keyword match on 'explore/audit/document' → discovery; 'then/chain/sequential' → linear; 'parallel/fan' → fan-out; else blank). This is called from SKILL.md via `node dist/scaffold.js` CLI hook.

waves.name: Generator installer
waves.description: Implement `packages/generator/src/install.ts` and wire `packages/generator/bin/install.js`. The installer's job: copy `packages/generator/skill/SKILL.md` and the `templates/` tree into `~/.claude/skills/relay-generator/`. Create the skills dir if missing. On subsequent installs, overwrite SKILL.md and templates/ (so upgrades work). Print a success line with the installed path and a one-liner instructing the user to restart Claude Code if it's already running. Also expose the dist build of `scaffold.ts` (task_62) inside the installed skill dir so SKILL.md can invoke it via Bash.
